title: DOS opens new investigation into Harvard’s use of international visas
description_html:
The New York Times reports that the Department of State has opened a new investigation into Harvard University’s compliance with the Student and Exchange Visitor Program (SEVP). Secretary of State Marco Rubio informed Harvard of the investigation in a letter. Rubio did not refer to any specific allegation of wrongdoing but claimed that the investigation was necessary “to assist the department in meeting its policy objectives.” Rubio gave Harvard a one-week deadline to produce university records related to the student visa program and stated that the Department of State plans to interview university staff and visa holders.

A State Department press release confirmed initiation of the investigation.
